impacts.description: Contribution to achieving EU open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies, as specified by the programme destination for this call.
Advancement of the European Apply AI Strategy through the development and validation of next-generation AI agents capable of real-world deployment in key economic sectors.
Strengthening the European AI R&D ecosystem through mandatory sharing of communicable project results via the AI-on-demand platform.
impacts.inference_note: Derived from the explicit requirement that communicable results be shared with the European R&D community via the AI-on-demand platform. Broad sharing of AI R&D results with the European research community constitutes a direct scientific ecosystem impact. Inference chain: mandatory dissemination obligation to the AI-on-demand platform → broad access to results for European researchers → strengthening of the European AI R&D ecosystem.

impacts.description: Societal impact in the healthcare sector through AI agent applications validated in real-world healthcare environments.
impacts.inference_note: Derived from the portfolio selection procedure requiring at least one funded project in the healthcare sector. The explicit sector targeting indicates an expected societal impact in healthcare; however, no dedicated expected impacts section in the source documents states this explicitly. Inference chain: portfolio selection requirement for healthcare sector project → AI agent solutions applied in healthcare environments → societal impact through improved healthcare outcomes.

impacts.description: Economic impact in the advanced manufacturing sector through AI agent solutions enhancing productivity and automation in manufacturing environments.
impacts.inference_note: Derived from the portfolio selection procedure requiring at least one funded project in the advanced manufacturing sector. The sector targeting indicates an intended economic impact in manufacturing; no explicit advanced manufacturing impact statement appears in the source documents. Inference chain: portfolio selection requirement for advanced manufacturing project → AI agent solutions deployed in manufacturing → economic impact through enhanced productivity and automation.

impacts.description: Technological impact in the autonomous driving sector through AI agent solutions for in-vehicle autonomous driving applications.
impacts.inference_note: Derived from the portfolio selection procedure requiring at least one funded project in the in-vehicle autonomous driving sector. The sector targeting indicates an intended technological impact in autonomous systems; no explicit autonomous driving impact statement appears in the source documents. Inference chain: portfolio selection requirement for autonomous driving project → AI agent solutions for in-vehicle systems → technological impact advancing autonomous driving capabilities.

impacts.description: Wider economic and digital transformation impact through deployment of AI agent solutions via European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs) and validation in Testing and Experiment Facilities (TEFs).
impacts.inference_note: Derived from the scope statement that project results 'may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities and further deployed via the European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs).' The EDIH network is a deployment pathway for economic and digital transformation across European SMEs and industry. The 'may' qualifier indicates this is optional, but its explicit mention signals an intended impact route. Inference chain: TEF validation → EDIH deployment → broader AI agent adoption across European industry → economic and digital transformation impact.
